question_text: While calculating Spearman’s rank correlation coefficient on n paired observations, it is found that xi are distinct for all i ≥2, with a single tie x1 = x2, and Pn i=1 d2 i = 19.5 where di = rank(xi) −rank(yi). Then the minimum possible value of n3 −n (in integer)
solution:
For ranks without considering tie corrections, the well–known upper bound is
i=1
i ≤n(n2 −1)
(attained when the two rankings are in reverse order). With only one tie in x the attainable
values may be half–integers, but the same upper bound still controls the sum.
We need the smallest n such that 19.5 ≤n(n2 −1)
. Check n = 3: 3(9 −1)
= 8 < 19.5. For
n = 4: 4(16 −1)
= 20 ≥19.5 (feasible due to the half–integer total from the single tie).
Hence the minimum n is 4, and therefore
n3 −n = 43 −4 = 64 −4 = 60 .
Quick Tip
Spearman’s P d2
i is maximized (and thus bounded) by n(n2 −1)/3. Use this bound to
find the smallest n compatible with a given P d2
i .
